subject: Re: Error during preprocessing
body:
Hi Jacob,

Unfortunately I have not been able to solve the problem.  Please do let me
know if you manage figure anything out!

Best,
Jake

On Sat, Aug 13, 2022 at 6:26 PM Jacob Anderson <jacronand13@gmail.com>
wrote:

> Hi Jacob,
>
> Did you end up solving this problem?  I ran into what could be a similar
> problem during preprocessing. I included a screenshot below.
>
> I tried troubleshooting each dashed likely reason. (1) I am running Warp
> using the only User on the machine (occurs w/ and w/o using administrator
> privlidges). (2) I made the processing range as large as possible so that
> more than one peak might occur in the CTF (3) The problem occurs with and
> without me inputting a gain reference.
>
> Curious if you found the problem!
>
>
> [image: Screenshot (7).png]
> On Monday, July 18, 2022 at 10:44:40 AM UTC-7 jac...@uw.edu wrote:
>
>> Hello,
>>
>> I'm getting an error during preprocessing which is resulting on all of
>> the micrographs turning grey and becoming deselected.  The error reads:
>>
>> Received "MovieProcessCTF", with 2 arguments, for GPU #0, 22941 MB free
>> System.OverflowException: Arithmetic operation resulted in an overflow.
>>    at Warp.Image.get_HostData() in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Image.cs:line 95
>>    at Warp.Image..ctor(int3 dims, Boolean isft, Boolean iscomplex,
>> Boolean ishalf) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Image.cs:line 243
>>    at Warp.Movie.ProcessCTF(Image originalStack,
>> ProcessingOptionsMovieCTF options) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Movie.cs:line
>> 1340
>>    at WarpWorker.Program.Main(String[] args) in
>> D:\Dev\warp2\WarpWorker\Program.cs:line 171
>> Received "LoadStack", with 3 arguments, for GPU #0, 22900 MB free
>> Loaded stack: 5760, 4092, 80, normal, real, 1 A/px, ID = -1, 0.5
>> 10.266
>>
>> Does anyone know what could be causing this?
>>
>> Thanks!
>> Jacob Croft
>> University of Washington
>>
> --

>
